title: Ice, Ice, Maybe: An Interactive Explainer for a Geometric Mechanism for the Hubble Tension
description: An interactive, single-file browser explainer accompanying the preprint "Ice, Ice, Maybe: A Geometric Mechanism for the Hubble Tension" (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20602920). In a Randall-Sundrum braneworld, a higher-dimensional bulk with a power-law matter profile imprints a brane term scaling as a^-n rather than the a^-4 of empty-bulk dark radiation. For 3 < n < 4 the component dilutes faster than matter but slower than radiation, peaking near matter-radiation equality. The visualisation walks through this at the level of the background expansion H(a) only. The figures are illustrative, not fitted to data, and the work does not claim to resolve the tension.
notes: Built on the modified Friedmann equation of Apostolopoulos and Tetradis (2004).
